title: gh-142029: Raise `ValueError` instead of crashing on empty name given to `create_builtin()`
author: dr-carlos
body:
<!--
Thanks for your contribution!
Please read this comment in its entirety. It's quite important.

# Pull Request title

It should be in the following format:

```
gh-NNNNNN: Summary of the changes made
```

Where: gh-NNNNNN refers to the GitHub issue number.

Most PRs will require an issue number. Trivial changes, like fixing a typo, do not need an issue.

# Backport Pull Request title

If this is a backport PR (PR made against branches other than `main`),
please ensure that the PR title is in the following format:

```
[X.Y] <title from the original PR> (GH-NNNNNN)
```

Where: [X.Y] is the branch name, for example: [3.13].

GH-NNNNNN refers to the PR number from `main`.

-->


<!-- gh-issue-number: gh-142029 -->
* Issue: gh-142029
<!-- /gh-issue-number -->

comments.author: dr-carlos
comments.body: This function isn't documented or tested anywhere already, so I haven't added a test.

comments.author: bedevere-app[bot]
comments.body:
<!-- changes-requested -->
A Python core developer has requested some changes be made to your pull request before we can consider merging it. If you could please address their requests along with any other requests in other reviews from core developers that would be appreciated.

Once you have made the requested changes, please leave a comment on this pull request containing the phrase `I have made the requested changes; please review again`. I will then notify any core developers who have left a review that you're ready for them to take another look at this pull request.
<!-- /changes-requested -->

comments.author: dr-carlos
comments.body:
I've added simple tests based on the errors thrown in `create_builtin()`.

I have made the requested changes; please review again

comments.author: bedevere-app[bot]
comments.body:
Thanks for making the requested changes!

@brettcannon: please review the changes made to this pull request.

comments.author: miss-islington-app[bot]
comments.body:
Sorry, @dr-carlos and @kumaraditya303, I could not cleanly backport this to `3.13` due to a conflict.
Please backport using [cherry_picker](https://pypi.org/project/cherry-picker/) on command line.
```
cherry_picker 70671267c11c562c6ca682eebf863616b258c20a 3.13
```
